CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-13-2015 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jun-01-2015 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jul-21-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendant'S Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement (Control Date)
Off Calendar May-21-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant's Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement is taken off-calendar. Dismissal filed. Judge: Lynn O'Malley Taylor; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/LOT)

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-12-2015 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-13-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JOSEPH W. STRELLA JR. OF CLAPP MORONEY, TEL. NO. (650)989-5400 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. CARIE A. EDWARDS OF THE VOGL MEREDITH LLP, TEL. NO. 398-0200 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS KAMEL BOUZIDI, AND ADAM AND SARA LLC DBA CAFE MED'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT - The motion for summary judgment and summary adjudication is denied. The court denies summary adjudication as to issues A-E, H, J, and L. Kamel's undisputed fact 7 states as a factual matter that Kamel signed the noncompete agreement on 5/16/13 and undisputed fact 8 states that he executed the purchase contract on 2/12/13. Jamel and Sofiene cite Kamel's responses to request for admission 13 and 17 where he denies signing the agreements and contends that they are forgeries. The cited evidence thus creates a disputed issue of material fact. In the reply, Kamel argues that he is simply assuming for the sake of this motion that the contracts are viable. Kamel, however, did not draft the separate statement to reflect that assertion. The motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issue F. Kamel is correct that there can be no tort recovery on this claim because this is not an insurance case. Kamel, however, failed to maintain his initial burden of production. Plaintiffs' evidence indicates that there is other conduct that does not parrot the breach of express contract cause of action that forms the basis for the claim. The court denies summary adjudication as to issues G, I, and K. Kamel argues that plaintiff's discovery responses are factually devoid and therefore, plaintiff cannot establish the interference claims. That argument fails because Kamel mischaracterizes the responses. While some of the responses could list more facts, several of them are factually rich. For example, response to special interrogatory 24 expressly cites the e-mails that support the claims. The court denies summary adjudication as to issue M. Kamel failed to maintain his initial burden of production. Kamel argues that plaintiffs cannot establish anti-competitive conduct, but he does not address all of plaintiff's claims. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-19-2015 Continued To Mar-13-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
